title: Training vs. Inference for Dropout
concept: Dropout is turned on during training to make learning more robust, then turned off during inference so predictions use the full network consistently.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is dropout doing during training?

options.text: Randomly turning off a fraction of neuron activations for each step.
Renaming the labels after each batch.
Keeping the same fixed subset of neurons off forever.

exercises.explain: Correct. Dropout temporarily mutes a changing random subset of neurons while the model trains.

cards.title: Practice under stress, perform at full strength
cards.body: A singer may rehearse with background noise or while wearing ear monitors that make practice harder. But on performance night, they sing with the full sound system working normally. Dropout follows the same logic: training is the tough rehearsal, inference is the real performance.

cards.title: Two modes, two rules
cards.body: During training, dropout is turned on so random neurons are muted and the model learns not to rely on fixed partnerships. During inference (prediction time), dropout is turned off so the full network is available and outputs stay stable from one prediction to the next.

cards.title: Weighted practice, normal race day
cards.body: A runner might train with extra resistance to build strength, then race without it. The harder practice builds resilience; the actual event uses full ability. Likewise, dropout makes training harder on purpose, but predictions should use the complete network.

cards.title: A spam detector in production
cards.body: A spam model may train for weeks with dropout active in hidden layers. But once it is deployed to score real emails, engineers turn dropout off. Otherwise, the same email could get slightly different answers every time because different neurons would keep disappearing.

cards.title: Turning dropout off is not cheating
cards.body: Some people worry that using all neurons at inference somehow breaks the training logic. It does not. The noisy training phase taught the network to be robust. The prediction phase simply uses that robust network in a steady, non-random way.

cards.title: Training mode vs. inference mode
cards.body: The same network behaves differently depending on whether it is learning or predicting.

left.text:
Dropout is on.
Random neurons are muted.
Robust learning is encouraged.

right.text:
Dropout is off.
All learned neurons can contribute.
Predictions stay consistent.

exercises.prompt: When should dropout usually be active?

options.text: During inference only, so predictions stay random.
During both training and inference forever.
Never, because robustness comes only from bigger datasets.

exercises.explain: Right. Dropout is a training-time challenge, not a prediction-time source of randomness.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Leaving dropout on during prediction can make the same input produce inconsistent outputs.
exercises.explain: True. If random neurons keep disappearing at inference time, outputs can wobble from run to run.

exercises.sentence: At inference time, dropout is usually turned ___.
exercises.explain: Correct. Predictions normally use the full network with dropout off.

exercises.prompt: Which deployment choice best fits dropout?

options.text: Train with dropout on so the model learns robustness, then switch dropout off when serving real predictions.
Turn dropout on only after deployment so users receive a fresh random answer each time.
Delete the dropout layer before training starts so the model never practices under noise.

exercises.explain: Exactly. The whole point is robust training followed by stable inference.

exercises.prompt: An app team notices that the same photo sometimes gets slightly different predictions from their trained model even when the input never changes. They discover dropout is still active in production. What is the best fix?

options.text: Turn dropout off during inference so the full network is used consistently for each prediction.
Increase the dropout rate even more so the randomness becomes stronger.
Delete all hidden layers because dropout and inference can never work together.
Change the labels on the training set to match the random outputs.

exercises.explain: Exactly. Dropout belongs to training mode, not prediction mode. Next, we will look at one subtle detail: how to keep the full network's output magnitude aligned with what the model practiced.
